issues.title: QuestionAnswerAdvisor: Augmentation of the user prompt with document context fails with structured output

comments.body: This is similar to what I mentioned in #2961 (see last comment). It was suggested to use a different delimiter so that the template renderer does not  fail with JSON.

@ThomasVitale Is this already fixed? Setting '<' and '>' as the delimiter token for the ChatClient and Advisor works, but the structured output feature should from my perspective  work in combination with the QuestionAnswerAdvisor, without any customizations.

Hi @timosalm, thank you for reporting. It is a regression and @ThomasVitale has already fixed via https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/2963 Please use the snapshot to verify and re-open if you still see the issue.

issues.title: VectorStoreChatMemoryAdvisor not able to retrieve chat pairs.
issues.body:
Version: 1.0.0.M5

I’m using the vectorstore chat memory advisor to create a conversational Api. 

I’m passing a conversation id in the advisor which stores all chats and responses in my vector table. 

Now, i need a way to retrieve all chats based on given conversation id. The problem is that the response is providing a list of messages for a conversation which i have to manually sort into user and assistant messages.  

Even if i sort them, there seems to be no way to get the chat pairs in response as I can’t find a way to relate which assistant message belongs to which user message.  

Is there a way to implement a chat_pair_id along with timestamps so i can form a proper chat history?

comments.body:
M5 is quite old. 

The new APIs in M8 have a repository implementation to retrieve all messages based on a conversation id.  Messages in a conversation will naturally include user and assistant messages - that is the back and forth interaction.  The new implementation includes a timestamp in  JdbcChatMemoryRepository.

Better docs are coming, but in the meantime you can look in https://github.com/ThomasVitale/llm-apps-java-spring-ai/tree/main/patterns/memory for some good examples.
Brilliant, thanks for that. Looks like a lot of issues in M5 are fixed in more recent versions so i'll plan to move my codebase to M8.

issues.title: Using `spring.ai.openai.chat.options.response-format.schema` results in a bad request
issues.body:
**Bug description**
According to the documentation, you can configure OpenAI's `response_format` through properties ([docs](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/openai-chat.html#_configuring_via_application_properties)). However, using the json schema related properties results in a 400 Bad request error:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "message": "Unknown parameter: 'response_format.schema'.",
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "param": "response_format.schema",
    "code": "unknown_parameter"
  }
}
```

**Environment**
Using `spring-ai-openai:1.0.0-M8` on Amazon Corretto 21 on macOS M1 Sequoia 15.4.1.

**Steps to reproduce**

1. Configure the properties as seen in the documentation:

```java
spring.ai.openai.chat.options.response-format.type=JSON_SCHEMA
spring.ai.openai.chat.options.response-format.name=MySchemaName
spring.ai.openai.chat.options.response-format.schema={"type":"object","properties":{"steps":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","properties":{"explanation":{"type":"string"},"output":{"type":"string"}},"required":["explanation","output"],"additionalProperties":false}},"final_answer":{"type":"string"}},"required":["steps","final_answer"],"additionalProperties":false}
spring.ai.openai.chat.options.response-format.strict=true
```

2. Run any `ChatClient` prompt, eg:

```java
@Bean
ApplicationRunner runner(ChatClient.Builder builder) {
    ChatClient client = builder.build();
    return args -> {
        String result = client.prompt().user("generate an object").call().content();
        logger.info(result);
    };
}
```

This results in the following exception:

```
org.springframework.ai.retry.NonTransientAiException: 400 - {
  "error": {
    "message": "Unknown parameter: 'response_format.schema'.",
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "param": "response_format.schema",
    "code": "unknown_parameter"
  }
}
	at org.springframework.ai.retry.autoconfigure.SpringAiRetryAutoConfiguration$2.handleError(SpringAiRetryAutoConfiguration.java:100) ~[spring-ai-autoconfigure-retry-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.web.client.ResponseErrorHandler.handleError(ResponseErrorHandler.java:58) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.web.client.StatusHandler.lambda$fromErrorHandler$1(StatusHandler.java:71) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.web.client.StatusHandler.handle(StatusHandler.java:146) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClient$DefaultResponseSpec.applyStatusHandlers(DefaultRestClient.java:831) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClient$DefaultResponseSpec.lambda$readBody$4(DefaultRestClient.java:820) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClient.readWithMessageConverters(DefaultRestClient.java:216) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClient$DefaultResponseSpec.readBody(DefaultRestClient.java:819) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClient$DefaultResponseSpec.lambda$toEntityInternal$2(DefaultRestClient.java:775) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClient$DefaultRequestBodyUriSpec.exchangeInternal(DefaultRestClient.java:579) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClient$DefaultRequestBodyUriSpec.exchange(DefaultRestClient.java:533) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.web.client.RestClient$RequestHeadersSpec.exchange(RestClient.java:680) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClient$DefaultResponseSpec.executeAndExtract(DefaultRestClient.java:814) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClient$DefaultResponseSpec.toEntityInternal(DefaultRestClient.java:774) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClient$DefaultResponseSpec.toEntity(DefaultRestClient.java:763) ~[spring-web-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.ai.openai.api.OpenAiApi.chatCompletionEntity(OpenAiApi.java:166) ~[spring-ai-openai-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel.lambda$internalCall$1(OpenAiChatModel.java:197) ~[spring-ai-openai-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.retry.support.RetryTemplate.doExecute(RetryTemplate.java:357) ~[spring-retry-2.0.11.jar:na]
	at org.springframework.retry.support.RetryTemplate.execute(RetryTemplate.java:230) ~[spring-retry-2.0.11.jar:na]

	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.DefaultChatClient$DefaultCallResponseSpec.doGetObservableChatClientResponse(DefaultChatClient.java:511) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.DefaultChatClient$DefaultCallResponseSpec.doGetObservableChatClientResponse(DefaultChatClient.java:494) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.DefaultChatClient$DefaultCallResponseSpec.content(DefaultChatClient.java:489) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at com.example.demo.DemoApplication.lambda$runner$0(DemoApplication.java:40) ~[classes/:na]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.lambda$callRunner$4(SpringApplication.java:785) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]
	at org.springframework.util.function.ThrowingConsumer$1.acceptWithException(ThrowingConsumer.java:82) ~[spring-core-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.util.function.ThrowingConsumer.accept(ThrowingConsumer.java:60) ~[spring-core-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.util.function.ThrowingConsumer$1.accept(ThrowingConsumer.java:86) ~[spring-core-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.callRunner(SpringApplication.java:797) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.callRunner(SpringApplication.java:785) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.lambda$callRunners$3(SpringApplication.java:773) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ForEachOps$ForEachOp$OfRef.accept(ForEachOps.java:184) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.SortedOps$SizedRefSortingSink.end(SortedOps.java:357) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.copyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:510) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.wrapAndCopyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:499) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ForEachOps$ForEachOp.evaluateSequential(ForEachOps.java:151) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ForEachOps$ForEachOp$OfRef.evaluateSequential(ForEachOps.java:174) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.evaluate(AbstractPipeline.java:234) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline.forEach(ReferencePipeline.java:596) ~[na:na]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.callRunners(SpringApplication.java:773) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.run(SpringApplication.java:325) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]

	at com.example.demo.DemoApplication.main(DemoApplication.java:18) ~[classes/:na]
```

**Expected behavior**
I expect Open AI to return a structured response according to the given JSON schema.

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
See code provided in steps to reproduce.

comments.body:
The issue seems to be caused because the `ResponseFormat` class is used both to deserialize the properties as to pass as part of the request body to OpenAI's API. To make that work, the `ResponseFormat.jsonSchema` property is constructed based on the string provided in `ResponseFormat.schema`([relevant code](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/7bb553ecce0a436406a9ed9b091c5d9ae545b9ea/models/spring-ai-openai/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/openai/api/ResponseFormat.java#L91-L96)). 

The result is that both `response_format.json_schema` (good) and `response_format.schema` (bad?) are sent to OpenAI's API.

A workaround is to construct the `ResponseFormat` programmatically, eg:

```java
ChatClient chatClient = builder
	.defaultOptions(OpenAiChatOptions.builder()
		.responseFormat(ResponseFormat.builder()
			.type(ResponseFormat.Type.JSON_SCHEMA)
			.jsonSchema(ResponseFormat.JsonSchema.builder()
				.schema("{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"steps\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"explanation\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"output\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"explanation\",\"output\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}},\"final_answer\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"steps\",\"final_answer\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}")
				.strict(true)
				.build())
			.build())
		.build())
	.build();
```

This allows you to construct it without setting the `ResponseFormat.schema` field.

See also:

* [Question asked on Stack Overflow on which I based this issue](https://stackoverflow.com/q/79602663/1915448)
* [Question asked on Reddit (by the same author)](https://www.reddit.com/r/SpringBoot/comments/1kcnebs/in_springai_100snapshot_why_am_i_getting_unknown/)

Great discovery...👍

The `schema` variable should only be used internally within the ResponseFormat class. However, it appears to have been assigned a value other than null, which prevents it from being filtered out by `@JsonInclude(Include.NON_NULL)` (refer to the `setSchema` method).

In my opinion, adding `@JsonIgnore` to the following part should resolve the issue:
```java
private String schema;
```

Would you like to contribute?

I've merged https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/2972 and did a brief review on the openai ref docs.  I think this solves the issue.  Thanks!  

If this doesn't resolve the issue, please reopen.
